title: Beyond Rational Illusion: Behaviorally Realistic Strategic Classification
core_claim: The paper reframes strategic classification to account for behaviorally realistic, prospect-theory-inspired agent responses instead of fully rational manipulation, and proposes Pro-SF to model and learn under that setting.
triage_rationale: This is a substantive ML theory paper with a concrete decision-and-response setup, and the behavioral-economics angle gives it more analytical value than a generic benchmark-only submission. It is not clearly tied to a real deployment workflow or finance-specific task, so it is better queued than advanced immediately.
likely_red_flags: Applied-domain signal appears weak relative to the abstract; the finance modeling tag is not substantiated by the title/abstract.
Claims of real-world reliability are broad for an abstract that only mentions synthetic and real-world datasets.
No clear evidence of expert baselines, leakage analysis, or deployment validation in the provided metadata.
